description: Application IDs referenced in cross-tenant access policy configuration

sharepoint_online.name: SharePoint Online / OneDrive for Business
sharepoint_online.usage: Scope partner B2B Collaboration inbound to SharePoint only
sharepoint_online.ctu_relevance: Primary application scope for all partner overrides

microsoft_rights_management.name: Microsoft Rights Management Service (OME)
microsoft_rights_management.usage: MUST be allowed in outbound settings to prevent encrypted email breakage
microsoft_rights_management.ctu_relevance: Critical — add to outbound allow list when implementing deny-by-default

microsoft_teams.name: Microsoft Teams
microsoft_teams.usage: Include in partner override if Teams access is needed beyond shared channels
microsoft_teams.ctu_relevance: May need to be added to partner overrides alongside SharePoint

office_365_exchange_online.name: Office 365 Exchange Online
office_365_exchange_online.usage: Include in partner override if Exchange/Outlook access is needed
office_365_exchange_online.ctu_relevance: Evaluate if cross-tenant email/calendar scenarios exist

power_bi_service.name: Power BI Service
power_bi_service.usage: Include in partner override for Power BI/Fabric access scenarios
power_bi_service.ctu_relevance: Relevant for bi-support-agent TLL Fabric access (Member userType in preview)

notes: App IDs are consistent across all Microsoft 365 tenants (global constants)
When scoping partner policies to specific apps, use these IDs in the applications section
The OME app ID is critical — forgetting it blocks encrypted email reading
Teams app ID may vary by deployment — verify in your tenant's enterprise applications
